settings.maxQSOTimeoutDesc: While in QSO (reply received, exchanging reports), max cycles to wait for the other party to respond
settings.maxCallAttemptsDesc: When actively calling another station, max retries if they don't respond
settings.displayPrefsDesc: Choose which operators to display on this client. Disabled operators won't appear in the UI and won't receive their events.

settings.hintDisabledHide: Disabled operators won't appear in the operator list
settings.hintDisabledEvents: Status updates and events for disabled operators won't be sent to this client

panel.title: Radio Control
panel.noRadioMode: No radio configured — radio control is unavailable

tuner_switch.label: Auto Tuner
tuner_switch.description: Enable or disable the radio's built-in antenna auto-tuner. When active, the radio automatically matches antenna impedance to reduce SWR and improve TX efficiency. Re-tune after changing bands or antennas.

tuner_tune.label: Manual Tune
tuner_tune.description: Trigger an immediate antenna matching cycle. Requires the auto-tuner to be enabled first. The radio will briefly transmit a low-power carrier during tuning — ensure an antenna is connected before pressing.

rf_power.label: TX Power
rf_power.description: Set transmit power from 0–100% of the radio's rated maximum. Reducing power is advisable for extended digital mode operation to prevent PA overheating; for voice, adjust freely based on propagation conditions.

af_gain.label: AF Gain
af_gain.description: Adjusts the audio output level of the radio's built-in speaker or headphone jack. This only affects the radio hardware volume, not the software monitor volume.

sql.label: Squelch
sql.description: Sets the receive squelch threshold. Higher values suppress weaker signals. For digital mode decoding, open squelch (0) is typically recommended; for voice, raise it to filter background noise as needed.

mic_gain.label: MIC Gain
mic_gain.description: Adjusts the microphone input preamplification level. Too high causes audio clipping and distortion; too low results in a weak transmitted signal. For voice, aim for the audio meter to just enter the yellow zone. Digital modes typically do not require adjustment.

nb.label: Noise Blanker
nb.description: The Noise Blanker (NB) suppresses impulse-type interference such as automotive ignition noise and industrial electrical interference. 0 means off; higher values increase blanking strength. Ineffective against continuous background noise — enable only when impulse interference is present.

nr.label: Noise Reduction
nr.description: Digital Signal Processing noise reduction (NR) reduces background white noise using DSP algorithms, improving weak signal readability. 0 means off. For FT8 and other digital mode decoding, set to 0 to avoid DSP processing affecting decode accuracy.
